title: SPOILERS MAYBE Oshii talks 30 translation needed
display_title: SPOILERS MAYBE Oshii talks 30 translation needed
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS MAYBE?] Oshii talks 3.0 (translation needed)             Quote Post by chee » Sun Dec 02, 2012 11:01 am Fresh from /a/: http://news.nicovideo.jp/watch/nw446903 translation please?

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Sun Dec 02, 2012 9:37 pm ...no one? This could be really interesting. ...yes I am entitled

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Mon Dec 03, 2012 10:35 am When I go to the page, google translates it for me. I don't even want to call the resulting product "rough"...

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Wed Dec 05, 2012 2:36 am I really admire Oshii, so I'd also love a translation from this text!

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Wed Dec 05, 2012 8:33 am Maybe we just need a thread for translations of interviews from the cast and crew as they come out?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Dec 05, 2012 5:39 pm This text might've been interesting if Oshii didn't pretty much start it with "oh btw I haven't seen any part of NME" (well okay, first he joked about mixing up/associating "Eva Q" with " Oba Q but that hardly counts). Needless to say it's absolute pain in the ass to actually translate because of difficult, technical language Oshii uses and there are sections I'm now entirely sure about (though that may be just because they seem to tie back to Oshii's theoretical wank about what "drama" means and since those rambles of his are mostly my difficulties with understanding what he is trying to get at may reflect nothing more than inherent obscurity of his thought) so I'm not getting down to doing translation right now , if ever. We'll see. I'd be more comfortable with symbv doing this anyway thanks to the difficulty level but I'm not sure if he is interested. As I immeaditly stated it's also 100% devoid of any interesting Q content, in fact it's pretty much 100% devoid of NME content at all. It's mostly just Oshii reflecting on Evangelion as work (in one section likening it to strange fusion of Meiji era naturalist literature and metafictional, deconstructive approaches) and its status, all of this of course mostly reflecting his own opinions on Eva (though he also approaches it from something akin "Anno's angle" often too). It's interesting read like most of Oshii's opinions are but little more than that and there's next to no meaningful NME content. What little there is to begin with is uninteresting and hard to take seriously given the guy has not seen a single minute of Rebuild. This could've been instance of simply "Oshii being Oshii" (like his insane as fuck comments on Ponyo that are difficult to judge in terms of how serious they are) but since apparently only reason he wrote the piece was due to editors bugging him to do it it doesn't look like he held even the interest he has in trolling Miyazaki in writing this.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Dec 05, 2012 9:14 pm Everything you just said makes me want to read it more

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Dec 06, 2012 6:50 am chee wrote: Everything you just said makes me want to read it more

[]:             Quote Post by FluffyBunny » Thu Dec 06, 2012 2:44 pm Xard wrote: One thing that weirded me out was his reference to only seeing "spring Eva" off Eva films which was simply wtf. "Spring Eva" is apparently way Japanese sometime refer to Death & Rebirth and the idea Oshii has only watched D&R but not EoE is just downright bizarre. Why would anyone (even Oshii) do that?
